summary: Completed frontend testing of timeframe chart scaling functionality. Found critical issue with 6M and 1M timeframes showing zoomed-in view instead of full period.

ui_bugs.issue: 6M and 1M timeframes show zoomed-in view (few candles) instead of full period
ui_bugs.details: When user selects 6M or 1M timeframe, chart shows only ~2 weeks of data instead of full 6 months/1 month period. 1Y timeframe works correctly showing full 2021-2026 range.

integration_issues.details: Long timeframes (1Y) use fitContent() correctly, but medium timeframes (6M, 1M) fall into wrong logic branch

passed_tests: Timeframe buttons are clickable and functional
Chart renders with proper canvas elements
1Y timeframe shows full historical data correctly (2021-2026)
4H timeframe shows detailed recent view as expected
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix timeframe zoom logic in ResearchChart.jsx. The issue is in the longTimeframes array (line 1504) - it includes '1M' and '6M' but the actual timeframe values passed are '30D' and '180D'. Update the array to use correct internal timeframe values: ['7D', '30D', '180D', '1Y'] instead of ['7D', '30D', '180D', '1Y', '1M', '6M'].

success_percentage.frontend: 70% - Core functionality works, but medium timeframes have scaling issues
